title: taraxacum officinale proles lanceolatum
intro: Taraxacum officinale proles lanceolatum, also known as the Lance-leaved Dandelion, is a subspecies of the common dandelion plant, characterized by its lance-shaped leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve liver and kidney problems. The health benefits of Lance-leaved Dandelion include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and act as a diuretic to reduce water retention. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of benefits and potential interactions with medications.
